column1: # Batch trade template (CSV). Drop a copy of this file (renamed without ".sample")
# into data/trades/ and the pricer will load every row as a trade.
# Columns:
#   trade_id                  trailing unique id number ONLY (e.g. 20496135). The full id
#                              AMEX_DAILY_IRS_<valuation date>_<id> is reconstructed by the pricer.
#   notional                  positive number
#   pay_fixed                 true = pay fixed/receive float
#   fixed_rate                decimal
#   start_date                ISO date
#   maturity_date             ISO date
column2: last accrual end (final pay = maturity + payment_delay_bdays)

column1: #   fixed_frequency           1Y / 6M / 3M / 1M / 1W / 1D
#   fixed_daycount            ACT/360 (default) | ACT/365F | 30/360 | 30E/360 | ACT/ACT-ISDA
#   floating_daycount         same options as fixed_daycount; usually ACT/360
#   floating_spread           decimal
#   fixed_principal_exchange  none (default) | start | end | both -- principal flows on fixed leg
#   floating_principal_exchange  same options -- principal flows on floating leg
#                                  start = -notional at start_date (paid out)
#                                  end   = +notional at final payment date (received)
#   fixing_calendar           NY_FED (only one supported today)
#   payment_calendar          NY_FED
#   payment_delay_bdays       int
column2: business-day lag between accrual end and cash payment

column1: #   lockout_bdays             int
column2: business days of frozen fixings at the period end

column1: #   business_day_convention   ModifiedFollowing (default) | Following | Preceding | ModifiedPreceding | Nearest | None | NoAdjust
#                                global fallback for the per-leg rolls below
#   --- Per-leg roll conventions (optional; blank => fall back to business_day_convention) ---
#   fixed_spot_roll           rolls the unadjusted effective/start date
#   fixed_accrual_roll        rolls each unadjusted accrual end on the fixed schedule
#   fixed_pay_roll            rolls each unadjusted payment date on the fixed leg
#   floating_accrual_roll     rolls each unadjusted accrual end on the floating schedule
#   floating_pay_roll         rolls each unadjusted payment date on the floating leg
#   floating_fixing_roll      rolls the lookback-shifted fixing date; default Preceding
#   floating_fixing_lag_bdays int business-day lookback for fixings; 0 = rate set in advance
#   description               free text (optional)
# Lines starting with # are ignored. Blank rows are ignored.
trade_id
